methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)

data.narrative:
Ursa Major Technologies is a Series E-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four private companies at Series D–E stage in the same sector: Astranis ($3.5B), ICEYE ($2.8B), Apex Space ($1.0B), and Stoke Space ($1.0B). Public comparables in Space & Aerospace (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trade at a median revenue multiple of 42.9x but cannot be applied without the target's revenue.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $400M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, yielding $560M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same-stage private companies produces a $1.9B implied value, with individual valuations ranging from $1.0B to $3.5B. The public-comp anchoring method cannot be executed due to the absence of disclosed revenue.

The two applicable methods imply a valuation range of $476M to $2.2B, with a median of $1.2B. The wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in the peer set: two peers valued at $1.0B versus one at $3.5B. Key uncertainties include Ursa Major's revenue scale relative to peers, recent operational milestones, and whether any recent funding has closed above the $400M threshold. The stage-progression floor and peer median provide anchors; the upper bound depends on whether the company's execution profile aligns with high-end comparables.
